content.claim: Students in 6- and 8-year gimnáziums achieve roughly 10% greater progress than similarly able, similarly socioeconomically-situated peers in 4-year gimnaziums, but the mechanism is contested—attributable to peer effects, teacher allocation, or genuine pedagogical structure—with original panel data not observing score evolution before grade 8
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Contested causality: research explicitly notes difficulty separating peer-effects, teacher-placement, and structural effects
Selection/admission period effects not fully accounted for in reported estimates
Study covers only two observed years of score evolution; long-term trajectory unknown
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
